--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-07-03) ---
[2018-07-03 16:30] rezyk: the click effect seems tricky to gauge at a glance (due to fission turrets) .. it would be nice if, when i first encounter the unit, it was a bit easier to tell how important the clicks are in terms of value
[2018-07-03 16:39] leary93: I think I agree with fission turrets making it feel hard to gauge
[2018-07-03 16:39] supergreyman (Greyman): I see 9HP loss as losing 9 Engineers, which is worth about 18 gold. Fission Turret is around 5.3 gold value, so it's around the same value. (Though technically, Engineer is kinda weak, so it might be slightly stronger to click)
[2018-07-03 16:40] supergreyman (Greyman): Xaetron costs 7HP to gain 5 Gauss Charges, which is around the same value too.
[2018-07-03 16:41] supergreyman (Greyman): It used to be 7HP for 7 Gauss Charges but got nerfed because the click is too good.
[2018-07-03 16:41] leary93: Im not sure that's the issue
[2018-07-03 16:41] leary93: I think the issue is when this is in the set, that you have too much variables to think about
[2018-07-03 16:41] leary93: if you're deciding wether to get it or not
[2018-07-03 16:42] leary93: it's not just the forcefields or the soak
[2018-07-03 16:42] supergreyman (Greyman): Too complicated of a click ability?
[2018-07-03 16:42] leary93: but also an additional click that has a not so easy to gauge click
[2018-07-03 16:42] leary93: Like, the value doesn't just change with how many forcefields you can get
[2018-07-03 16:42] leary93: but also with your highest absorber
[2018-07-03 16:42] leary93: and engi availlability
[2018-07-03 16:43] leary93: as well as green sinks
[2018-07-03 16:43] supergreyman (Greyman): The click ability is an afterthought, which isn't part of my original design. So I guess I should remove it.
[2018-07-03 16:44] leary93: you could make it simpler, maybe more self-sufficient, by having it create 3 forcefields?
[2018-07-03 16:45] rezyk: i thought paying health with click was okay as a way to get value out of excess regen, just that the fission turrets were something that takes more experience to value (as a relatively new player, i'm not even sure yet how much of fission value lies in their click)
[2018-07-03 16:45] leary93: even as an experienced player
[2018-07-03 16:46] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, it feels too unrelated.
[2018-07-03 16:46] leary93: fission is one of the less obvious unit to me
[2018-07-03 16:46] rezyk: i wonder if there was a strong reason to not have it just create gauss charges?
[2018-07-03 16:46] leary93: too much Resophore-like?
[2018-07-03 16:46] leary93: I like the idea more of just being a superbig wall
[2018-07-03 16:47] supergreyman (Greyman): I somewhat want the click ability to go along with its Start of turn ability. Fission Turrets lets you get green for more Forcefields.
[2018-07-03 16:47] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): Fission is good even if you don't click it
[2018-07-03 16:47] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): Fission is good even if you don't click it
[2018-07-03 16:47] leary93: Doesn't make it easier to gauge
[2018-07-03 16:47] supergreyman (Greyman): But that's a lot more complicated than just making Forcefields.
[2018-07-03 16:47] leary93: it's more like: how good is fission "exactly"  this game
[2018-07-03 16:47] rezyk: how about just getting green directly?
[2018-07-03 16:48] leary93: Forcefields directly makes more sense?
[2018-07-03 16:48] leary93: paying health to get green needs a massive payout
[2018-07-03 16:48] leary93: at which point it's like: "why"
[2018-07-03 16:49] rezyk: i like forcefields directly thematically .. but mechanically you're paying BV to get BV, it might be a little too straightforward whether you should click or not?
[2018-07-03 16:51] rezyk: i guess you could always balance it out by adding resource costs if warranted
[2018-07-03 16:51] leary93: But the question is if you want to soak with the fields
[2018-07-03 16:51] leary93: instead of use them to regen
[2018-07-03 16:52] leary93: And I mean, Xaetron is straightforward too, is that bad?
[2018-07-03 16:52] leary93: I like straightforward a lot
[2018-07-03 16:53] rezyk: straightforward is good for player, but can be problematic if it doesn't provide enough levers for the designer to fine tune balance (not necessarily the case here, just musing)
[2018-07-03 16:53] supergreyman (Greyman): Like, if you're paying, let's say 6HP for 3 Forcefields, you would trade away excess HP for more regenerating HP (which would happen if opponent isn't ramping early attacks). It becomes a bit too easy to stall. (Although I can lower the lifespan)
[2018-07-03 16:54] leary93: I don't mind stalling if it requires investment to get there and can be punished in time
[2018-07-03 16:54] leary93: and ohhh yea this can
[2018-07-03 16:55] supergreyman (Greyman): The punishment for gaining regeneration is to sacrifice economy for Forcefield.
[2018-07-03 16:55] leary93: I think there's a really fine line
[2018-07-03 16:55] leary93: and it offers a lot of tactical depth in other ways than just: Do I click or not
[2018-07-03 16:57] supergreyman (Greyman): I think click for green is a bit better since it becomes a lot less obvious of whether you want to click or not.
[2018-07-03 16:58] leary93: I really dislike that
[2018-07-03 16:58] leary93: not just because it makes it less obvious

[2018-07-05 01:33] swapgo: Ebb costs 6 :Blue: for the same net money gain, and also has an upside instead of a downside that gives you a bit more economic burst. Granted, the G cost and the lower upfront cost maybe lets you get a sharper opening if the set is really aggressive. Ebb is also more breachproof (Ish) since you won't be clicking Unstable Reactor more than necessary
[2018-07-05 01:34] swapgo: Thorium doesn't actually costs G, costs a total of 8 (5 upfront, 3 from lost drones during buildtime) for 2 more Gold and an inbuilt Conduit. Provides tech sink and consistently breachproof
[2018-07-05 01:36] swapgo: Your design is actually more expensive than buying 2 drones when Energy is free, and it's two drones that you have to feed in the late game, a lot

BEHOLD. MORE DRONES. Also apologies for three units.
[2018-07-07 05:42] kaelii: Beta and gamma probably need the most work.
[2018-07-07 05:43] supergreyman (Greyman): :NotLikeThis: :NotLikeThis: :NotLikeThis:
[2018-07-07 05:45] kaelii: Actually thinking about it, beta definitely is weak right now. 3BBEE for a non-prompt wall and giving your opponent a drone is almost certainly not worth.
[2018-07-07 05:55] rezyk: pretty expensive upfront cost for purchases that are net negative on average
[2018-07-07 05:58] supergreyman (Greyman): This is just really confusing... 😄
[2018-07-07 05:58] supergreyman (Greyman): Alpha looks too strong, simply for the fact that it's too accessible for such a strong click ability.
[2018-07-07 06:00] supergreyman (Greyman): But other than that, I think it does create a lot of negative effect since it can create strong units for your opponent.
[2018-07-07 06:03] kaelii: I guess I could remove the drone cost on beta/gamma and limit supply down to a reasonable number for them all, and probably do some more thinking about how to buff gamma as it gives an alpha which is currently the strongest?
[2018-07-07 06:04] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): I pose the following: since Alpha gives Beta gives Gamma gives Alpha, they are all equal in cost, and you lose a drone but your opponent doesn't, the only way it would be worth buying any of them is if one is massively over/underpowered.
[2018-07-07 06:04] rezyk: my initial thought on making the opponent gift interesting and easing up on your cost is to replace the click payments with the purchase effects
[2018-07-07 06:05] rezyk: buy an alpha, click it for an effect but giving your opponent a beta.. he clicks, giving you a gamma...
[2018-07-07 06:05] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): There is no cycle on click, only on purchase
[2018-07-07 06:05] supergreyman (Greyman): That would cause infinite unit if you move it to click effect.
[2018-07-07 06:06] rezyk: right, i'm suggesting to move purchase effect/cycle to the click
[2018-07-07 06:06] kaelii: He's suggesting moving the gift into the click instead of purchase -- they'd need to be self-sac entirely I think then?
[2018-07-07 06:06] rezyk: maybe sac on click too, to avoid infinite (though you have that problem with the wall generation already)
[2018-07-07 06:06] kaelii: Beta has stamina currently -- makes limited number of wall.
[2018-07-07 06:07] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): that's way over the complexity budget and if you have one of them in a +11 set...
[2018-07-07 06:07] rezyk: i figured we blew that budget already 😃
[2018-07-07 06:08] supergreyman (Greyman): At least try to make 2 units instead of 3. Easy way to chop down 1/3 of the complexity.

[2018-07-07 06:38] supergreyman (Greyman): You might still need the red tech for something else, like Smorcus click for example.
[2018-07-07 06:40] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Makes the animus breachproof, and acts as an RR cost without breaking the challenge rules :Jappa:
[2018-07-07 06:41] supergreyman (Greyman): It's also to prevent Nitrocybe spam in early game.

[2018-07-07 06:58] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Personally I believe carrying units is great design.  Some people they it increases complexity by making cards more wordy, but the truth is it simplifies the game by having fewer units on the board at the same time!
